summary: Packaged desktop compute-node startup hung or failed before Registered: yes across an original llama_cpp discovery hang, a child import watchdog timeout, and a subprocess facade early exit. A follow-up identified stale site-packages pathlib.py stdlib shadowing as the concrete Windows facade import root cause.
impact: Windows packaged CUDA operators could fail before relay registration, and the shared packaged import/facade seam also risked macOS Metal parity.
fix: Reused desktop runtime setup probe diagnostics, normalized extended Windows paths in subprocess envs, made the subprocess facade import/model-init path report JSON errors, replaced the legacy EOF marker with an actionable exited-before-JSON-handshake diagnostic, omitted protocol JSON stdout payloads from diagnostic tails, and kept lifecycle tests gated on registered=true. Follow-up hardening keeps stdlib ahead of site-packages in packaged bootstrap/probe/facade paths, detects guarded stdlib shadowing, and adds polluted pathlib e2e fixtures against real relay.py registration.
lessons: Packaged e2e must prove registered=true for the full desktop + relay lifecycle and simulate native-runtime child failure shapes. Any child native-extension runtime path must share the successful probe bootstrap contract and surface child stderr/stdout/exit code on early exit without logging TOKEN_PLACE_LLAMA_CPP_JSON protocol payloads, prompts, completion chunks, or generated text. CI fixtures must also emulate polluted system site-packages backports because PYTHONNOUSERSITE does not protect stdlib from interpreter-level site-packages shadowing when code manually reorders sys.path.
